key_highlights: Global markets are in a sharp sell-off mode driven by surging Treasury yields (30-year at ~20-year high), inflation jitters, and geopolitical uncertainty following Trump's China trip with no major trade deals and ambiguous Taiwan stance.
Crude oil prices have surged to $105/barrel amid fears of prolonged supply disruption linked to the Iran conflict, raising stagflation risks.

The AI sector remains a bright spot with Cerebras's blockbuster IPO (surged 90% on debut) and continued bullishness on Microsoft (Ackman), Marvell, and cybersecurity stocks, but bond sell-off threatens to derail the AI frenzy.
Central bank divergence is evident: ECB signals modest rate hikes while Fed faces internal dissent (Powell named temporary chair amid disagreement), and global bonds are battered.
Key risks include a potential $200 oil shock, escalating geopolitical tensions (Iran, Taiwan, China), and a looming liquidity crunch for AI mega-IPOs.

sectors.trend: Mixed: AI mega-IPOs (Cerebras) and strong earnings (Marvell, Akamai) drive selective bullishness, but broader tech is under pressure from rising yields and inflation fears. AI data centers employ few people, raising sustainability questions.
sectors.implications: Investors should focus on AI infrastructure plays (Marvell, Akamai) and companies with strong fundamentals. Cerebras's pullback after IPO surge suggests caution on frothy valuations. Bond sell-off could be a headwind for high-growth tech.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum: Crude at $105/barrel due to Iran war supply fears, Nat-gas up on warm US temps, and Adnoc loading LNG onto tankers in Persian Gulf. Global inventory race intensifies.
sectors.implications: Energy stocks and commodities are clear beneficiaries. Cameco (uranium) also offers a play on energy volatility. However, $200 oil shock scenario poses systemic risk to global economy.

sectors.trend: Treasury yields surging (30-year at ~20-year high), dollar rallying for fifth straight day, global bonds battered. Fed rate cut hopes fading. ECB signals modest hikes.
sectors.implications: Rising yields are a headwind for equities, especially growth and tech. Dollar strength pressures EM currencies (India tightens silver import rules). Bond investors should shorten duration and consider TIPS.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Magnum Ice Cream surges on takeover speculation, mortgage applications up 21% YoY despite rising rates (homebuilder stocks benefit). Subscription fatigue and inflation weigh on consumer sentiment.
sectors.implications: Selective opportunities in homebuilders (Lennar, D.R. Horton) and defensive consumer staples. Avoid overvalued discretionary names. Watch for subscription model disruption.

sectors.trend: Freight market growing, driver demand squeezing large carriers (SONAR). Berkshire Hathaway returns to airlines with $2.6B Delta stake. NY commuter rail strike strands 300k daily riders.
sectors.implications: Delta (DAL) is a buy on Berkshire's vote of confidence. Rail strike highlights infrastructure vulnerability. Freight growth supports trucking and logistics names.

sectors.trend: Ebola outbreak in Congo declared global health emergency (rare strain, no vaccine). Dexcom makes lofty promise on continuous glucose monitoring, analysts bullish.
sectors.implications: Biotech and vaccine developers (e.g., Moderna, Gilead) could see short-term interest. Dexcom (DXCM) is a strong buy on innovation. Watch for travel restrictions and supply chain impacts from Ebola.

sectors.trend: Brookfield expands beyond real assets into AI and venture debt. NextEra Energy in talks to acquire Dominion. Zillow CEO preparing for next real estate revolution.
sectors.implications: Brookfield (BAM) offers diversified exposure to AI infrastructure. NextEra (NEE) is a defensive utility play with growth. Zillow (ZG) could benefit from housing market recovery.

key_themes: Geopolitical Risk: Trump's China trip yields no major deals, ambiguous on Taiwan, Iran war escalates, global inventory race intensifies.
Inflation & Bond Yields: 30-year yield at ~20-year high, dollar rally, Fed rate cut hopes fading, ECB cautious.
AI & Tech Divergence: AI mega-IPOs (Cerebras, SpaceX) attract massive demand, but bond sell-off threatens to derail the frenzy.
Energy Crisis: Oil at $105, potential $200 shock, Nat-gas up, global supply disruption fears.
Central Bank Policy: Fed internal dissent (Powell temporary chair), ECB modest hikes, global monetary tightening continues.
Labor & Migration: Spain warns bars would shut without migrants, India tightens silver import rules to defend rupee, UK pound to PKR rate weakens.
Consumer Strain: Subscription fatigue, inflation jitters, mortgage rates rising but applications up 21% YoY.
Space & Defense: SpaceX IPO timeline accelerated (June 12), Boeing eases on China order, space stocks rally.
Cybersecurity: Akamai upgraded, cybersecurity giant unlocks new buy zone, Salesforce wins $72M Air Force contract.
Commodities: Gold falls toward $4,550 as Fed-cut hopes fade, silver import rules tightened in India, copper and uranium in focus.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase cash allocation, hedge with gold and energy stocks, reduce exposure to EM equities and currencies.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Sustained Bond Yield Surge & Dollar Strength
risk_assessment.mitigation: Shorten bond duration, overweight TIPS, reduce growth/tech exposure, increase allocation to value and financials.
Overweight energy stocks (XOM, CVX, Cameco), consider commodity ETFs, reduce consumer discretionary exposure.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI Bubble Burst / Liquidity Crunch for Mega-IPOs
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on frothy AI names, focus on profitable AI infrastructure plays (Marvell, Akamai), avoid overvalued pre-revenue IPOs.
Increase defensive allocations (utilities, healthcare, consumer staples), consider short-term Treasuries, reduce cyclical exposure.
Avoid companies with high labor exposure (e.g., rail, airlines), monitor freight rates, consider logistics and automation plays.
Monitor travel restrictions, consider biotech ETFs, avoid airlines and travel stocks if outbreak spreads.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Delta Air Lines (DAL) on Berkshire's $2.6B stake
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's return to airlines signals value and cyclical recovery. Delta has strong pricing power and benefits from growing freight market.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Marvell Technology (MRVL) on AI data center growth
opportunities.rationale: Marvell has a hidden growth engine in custom AI chips and networking. Strong earnings and analyst upgrades support upside.
Oil at $105 and potential $200 shock make energy the best inflation hedge. Cameco benefits from nuclear renaissance and uranium demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Dexcom (DXCM) on innovation and analyst bullishness
opportunities.rationale: Dexcom's continuous glucose monitoring promise and strong analyst sentiment make it a leader in medtech. Defensive growth in healthcare.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Homebuilder Stocks (LEN, DHI) on mortgage application surge
opportunities.rationale: Mortgage applications up 21% YoY despite rising rates indicate resilient housing demand. Homebuilders benefit from supply constraints.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Akamai (AKAM) on analyst upgrade and cybersecurity pivot
opportunities.rationale: Akamai is no longer legacy tech; it's a cybersecurity and edge computing play. Upgrade signals strong growth potential.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 20-30% of portfolio
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty, bond yield surge, and potential oil shock warrant higher liquidity to deploy during dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Shorten bond duration and overweight TIPS
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and inflation make long-duration bonds risky. TIPS provide inflation protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with gold and commodity ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold is falling but remains a safe haven. Commodity ETFs (DBC, GSG) benefit from inflation and supply disruptions.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to EM equities and currencies
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar strength and geopolitical risks (India, China) pressure EM. India's silver import rules and AI miss signal headwinds.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Take partial profits on AI mega-IPO names (Cerebras)
defensive_moves.rationale: Cerebras surged 90% on debut but is now pulling back. Lock in gains and wait for better entry on pullbacks.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish. Markets are likely to remain volatile with a downward bias due to rising bond yields, inflation fears, and geopolitical uncertainty (Iran, China/Taiwan). Oil at $105 could push inflation higher, delaying Fed rate cuts. The AI frenzy may cool as liquidity tightens. Defensive sectors (energy, healthcare, utilities) will outperform. Key support levels for S&P 500 could be tested.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously bullish. Once inflation peaks and geopolitical tensions de-escalate, markets could recover. AI infrastructure spending (data centers, chips) remains a multi-year growth driver. SpaceX IPO and other mega-IPOs could reignite risk appetite. However, a $200 oil shock or recession would delay recovery. Long-term investors should focus on quality, cash-flow-rich companies.
key_catalysts: SpaceX IPO filing (June 12) and listing on Nasdaq.
Fed policy decision and Powell's commentary on inflation and rates.
Iran conflict resolution or escalation (impact on oil prices).
Trump's China trade deal details and Taiwan stance.
Ebola outbreak containment and global health response.
AI mega-IPOs (Cerebras, others) secondary offerings and lock-up expirations.
ECB rate decision and forward guidance.
US jobs and CPI data for May/June.
Berkshire Hathaway's continued airline and energy investments.
watch_list: Dollar index (DXY) strength vs. EM currencies.
Vodafone Idea (India) profitability and ARPU trends.
Mortgage rates and homebuilder stocks (LEN, DHI).
Ebola case counts and travel restrictions.
AI data center employment and capex reports.
